mnemonics.title: DISTRIBUTED MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: DISTRIBUTED STANDS FOR: Decentralized, Independent computers, Synchronized through network, Transparent to users, Reliable and fault-tolerant, Uptime guaranteed, Distributed computing, Energy-efficient, Distributed resources, Embedded in multiple locations.

mnemonics.title: CAP THEOREM MEMORY
mnemonics.content: CAP: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance - CHOOSE ANY TWO. Remember: When there's a partition (P), you must choose between consistency (C) and availability (A).

mnemonics.title: TRANSPARENCY TYPES
mnemonics.content: 6 TRANSPARENCIES: ALFIR - Access, Location, Failure, Implementation, Replication. Use sentence: 'APPLICATIONS LOVE FINDING INTERESTING REPLICAS.'

acronyms.usage: Allows calling functions on remote machines as if they were local
Distributed file system protocol for sharing files over networks
Synchronizes clocks of computers over a network
File system that spans multiple machines in a network
Decentralized network architecture where nodes share resources directly
Fundamental theorem describing trade-offs in distributed systems
Java's version of RPC for method calls on remote objects

visualTips.visualization: Draw a central server with multiple client computers connected in a star pattern, showing arrows from clients to server for requests and back for responses.
Draw a circular arrangement of equal-sized nodes with bidirectional arrows between all nodes, showing each can request and respond to others.
Draw multiple clocks at different positions with arrows indicating synchronization messages flowing to a central time server or between peers.
Draw two sets of process-resource arrows crossing between machines, forming a cycle: P1 holds R1 wants R2, P2 holds R2 wants R1, across different computers.
Draw a primary database at center with multiple identical copies (replicas) surrounding it, with arrows showing synchronization between them.

analogies.analogy: Like a chain of restaurants where each location has its own kitchen but customers perceive it as one restaurant with consistent menu and service.
Like a universal translator at an international conference, hiding language differences so participants can communicate without knowing others' languages.
Like an experienced driver focusing on the road without thinking about engine mechanics - the complexity is hidden.
Like orchestra musicians following a conductor to ensure all play in sync, even though they are at different positions.
Like a committee decision where all members must agree on a proposal, even when some may be absent or unreliable.
